text: Databases are systems that store and organize data. The two main types are SQL and NoSQL. SQL databases, like MySQL and PostgreSQL, use tables with rows and columns, similar to spreadsheets. They require a fixed structure, called a data model, where you define columns and data types in advance. To retrieve information, you write queries using SQL language. NoSQL databases, such as MongoDB, are more flexible and store data as documents or key-value pairs. They work well with changing data structures. Both types use indexes, which are special data structures that speed up queries by allowing the database to find information faster without scanning every record.
